title: After-Action Report — Foul-Out Leaderboards, XO Crossover Consistency, and RA/27
scope: The persistent Walk-Back runner rewrite shipped on this same branch and has its own deep-dive: `docs/aar-walk-back-persistent-runner.md`. This report covers the three stat-layer changes that rode alongside it.

rationale.item: "Foul out rates should probably be a leaderboard stat but I can't decide if they are a type of strikeout or a separate thing." … "It goes on both — I want to know how often hitters do it and how much pitchers do that too."

XO crossover: "The crossover stats are [anchoring] instead of actual batting average… you need to use batting average as a one-to-one, not plate average, which is different." The XO z-anchor is only correct if the per-player value and the league baseline distribution are computed by the same formula; they had drifted because _compute_xo_league_baselines re-derives each stat with its own hand-coded SQL loop. The failure mode is silent.

RA/27: "I'm wondering if there needs to be an out-share-type ERA rendering against runs allowed?" It matters in O27 specifically because the Walk-Back rule manufactures unearned runs by design — real runs that ERA deliberately excludes.

delegation_events.decision: Foul-outs surfaced on both batting and pitching boards
delegation_events.quote: "It goes on both — I want to know how often hitters do it and how much pitchers do that too."

delegation_events.decision: Fix AVG anchoring and all other drifted XO baselines
Ship both native RA/27 and the ERA-anchored XO twin

actions.description: Foul-out leaderboards: Batting · Power & Counting FO + FO% (FO/PA); Pitching · Counting & Value FO; Pitching · Workload & Stuff FO% (FO/BF) beside K%; glossary entries for all four

_compute_xo_league_baselines now mirrors the per-player formula for every XO stat: AVG → H/AB (bavg), wOBA → dynamic weights + stay split, BABIP → strikeout-excluded denominator, oSLG/oOPS → (H+3·HR)/AB_faced; comments at both sites stating they MUST stay in sync
Native RA/27 in _aggregate_pitcher_rows: runs_allowed × 27 / outs, shown beside wERA and xRA
ERA-anchored XO twin: ra27 added to XO_PITCHER_STATS with the MLB ERA anchor (4.30 / 1.05) and matching baseline distribution; glossary entry + two leaderboard cards (native + XO)

risk_posture: Kept foul-outs as their own stat rather than folding them into the strikeout counters, matching how the DB models them — while K% continues to blend them in (`(K + foul-outs) / BF`), the established O27 convention.
ERA/WHIP/K9/BB9/HR9 were already consistent and untouched.

validation_claims.claim: mean(xo) over qualified players now lands on the MLB anchor for all six batter stats and the opponent-slash pitcher stats.
validation_claims.check_method: Compute mean XO over qualified players and compare to anchors

validation_claims.claim: RA/27 ≥ ERA for every pitcher (total ≥ earned, as it must be). Native league mean ~19–22 vs ERA ~15. XO RA/27 centers on 4.302 (target 4.30).
validation_claims.check_method: Audit RA/27 vs ERA per pitcher and league means

validation_claims.claim: Suites run clean (render / analytics / park / walk-back); the only repo failures are the pre-existing test_phase8_db_migration idempotency cases and a flaky random trade-noise test, both unrelated and red on the base commit.
validation_claims.check_method: Run pytest suites and compare to base commit

non_actions.item: Extracting a single shared rate-from-counts helper so baseline and per-player formulas can't drift
non_actions.reason: deferred because _aggregate_*_rows calls back into _league_baselines, risking recursion

non_actions.item: Revisiting whether the K-excluding PA-based denominator is the "right" BABIP for O27
non_actions.reason: a separate modeling question, untouched here

residual_risks: The crossover baseline and the per-player stat are two code paths computing the same quantity; any formula divergence silently miscalibrates the translation.
open_hooks: Longer-term cleanup: extract a single shared rate-from-counts helper
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Foul-Out Leaderboards, XO Crossover Consistency, and RA/27
unmapped_content:
**Branch:** `claude/add-foul-outs-stat-OXRYr`

XO formula: xo = MLB_mean + ((value − O27_mean) / O27_sd) × MLB_sd. Drift specifics: AVG per-player fed b["avg"] aliased to PAVG (H/PA) but baseline was H/AB; wOBA baseline used fixed MLB-ish weights vs dynamic empirical O27 weights with a true-singles/stay split; BABIP baseline (H−HR)/(AB−HR) vs per-player (H−HR)/(PA−K−BB−HBP−HR); pitcher oSLG/oOPS baseline used full total bases vs crude (H+3·HR)/AB_faced. RA/27 user quote: "Or we can use RA/27 since [we] already have wERA or xRA… what makes sense?" The trio reads together — wERA (arc-weighted earned), xRA (expected), RA/27 (all runs) — so RA/27 − wERA is the run damage the earned-run stats hide; XO RA/27 − XO ERA is the same cost on the MLB ERA scale. Foul-out data already existed; only the surfacing was missing.
parser_notes: Clear AAR; references sibling AAR docs/aar-walk-back-persistent-runner.md on the same branch.
